codeReview.summary: The quarter-Kelly bankroll module covers the requested sizing, 5% cap, same-team ML/RL collapse, SQLite ledger updates, and drawdown/summary tracking. I found one non-blocking API-integrity edge case: settling a bet without an existing pending row will insert a settled bet and adjust the bankroll, so the public helper can mint balance if called out of sequence, but the current in-repo settlement path only settles pending bets and the contracted behavior is otherwise satisfied.

issues.description: `update_bankroll(action='settle', ...)` accepts settlements with no matching pending bet because `_upsert_settled_bet()` inserts a fresh settled row when none exists, and the caller then credits `current_bankroll + settlement_amount` (see lines 334-362 and 520-527). That can inflate bankroll/accounting if this public API is ever called out of order; the current `settlement.py` path is safe because it only loads pending bets first.

summary: Pass with one non-blocking issue: the feature meets the t22 contract and its tests/diff align with the expected bankroll behavior, but `update_bankroll()` should ideally reject settle-without-place misuse to harden ledger integrity.
